function.name: mcp_agentcall_configure_inbound_ai
function.description:
Set up an AI voice agent to autonomously answer incoming calls to a phone number. This is the AgentCall equivalent of Twilio's inbound voice webhook URL: instead of pointing the number at a webhook you host, you give AgentCall a system prompt and AgentCall handles the call with a managed AI voice agent. Use this (NOT 'create_webhook') when you want a phone number to answer calls. Set the system prompt, voice, and optional first message; when someone calls the number, the AI picks up and follows the prompt. Pass `notify.emailTo` to also receive a plain-English summary of every call (caller, intent, urgency, callback time) so small-business owners do not need to read transcripts. Free tier: 5 minutes/month free trial, no card required. Once the trial is exhausted, inbound AI calls hang up until the 1st of next month (UTC) or until the user upgrades to Pro for unlimited inbound AI at $0.40/min. Available on US and Canada numbers. Before writing systemPrompt from scratch, fetch GET https://api.agentcall.co/v1/calls/prompt-templates (no auth) for pre-built templates: receptionist, lead qualifier, appointment booker, customer support, call screener. Preview voices via GET https://api.agentcall.co/v1/calls/voices.

IMPORTANT (persistent config: confirm before enabling, monitor after): this is NOT a one-shot tool call. Once configured, the number autonomously answers every incoming call from then on, accumulates per-minute charges (at $0.40/min on Pro past the Free 5-minute monthly trial), and keeps doing so until disable_inbound_ai is called. Before invoking, get the user's explicit confirmation on: (1) exact E.164 number to configure (read it back), (2) systemPrompt content (read back at least the WHAT WE DO and PRICING sections so the user can correct invented facts before any caller hears them), (3) record setting (default false; recording adds $0.01/min and 1-year retention), (4) notify.emailTo (confirm the address is theirs and that they want every call summarized), (5) maxDurationSecs (use the smallest value that fits the use case so a stuck call cannot run for an hour), (6) budget expectations (Free 5 min/month then hang-up, or Pro $0.40/min with no monthly cap). After enabling, periodically check get_usage so the user sees consumption, and proactively offer to call disable_inbound_ai when their stated goal is met (testing finished, campaign over, business closed for the season). On reports of unexpected charges or unexpected calls, disable_inbound_ai first then debug.

numberId.description: The phone number ID to configure for inbound AI (e.g. 'num_abc123')

systemPrompt.description: Instructions for the AI when it answers (e.g. 'You are the front desk for Acme Plumbing. Greet callers, take their name, phone, and a brief description of the issue, then say someone will call back within 2 hours.')

voice.description: Voice: 10 options. marin (soft, natural) and cedar (warm, grounded) are the newest natural-sounding picks. shimmer (default), sage, ash, ballad, coral, echo, verse, alloy. Preview at GET /v1/calls/voices.

firstMessage.description: What the AI says when it picks up (e.g. 'Hi, thanks for calling Acme Plumbing. How can I help?')

maxDurationSecs.description: Max call duration in seconds (default 600, max 3600)

record.description: Opt in to call recording. When true, every inbound call to this number is recorded and listenable from the dashboard. Adds $0.01/min on top of the AI voice rate. Tell the user to make sure their first message includes a TCPA disclosure ('this call may be recorded for quality') — required by two-party-consent states. Default false.

emailTo.description: Email address to receive the post-call summary

businessName.description: Business name shown in the email subject and body

agencyName.description: Agency/brand name used in the email sign-off

notify.description: Optional post-call email notification. After every call, AgentCall summarizes the transcript with an LLM and emails the summary to the configured address. Spam calls are auto-suppressed. Email-only for v1; SMS notifications are not exposed.

function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

REPLACE MODE (mode='replace', default): find a unique string and replace it. REQUIRED PARAMETERS: mode, path, old_string, new_string.
PATCH MODE (mode='patch'): apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes. REQUIRED PARAMETERS: mode, patch.

mode.description: Edit mode. 'replace' (default): requires path + old_string + new_string. 'patch': requires patch content only.

path.description: REQUIRED when mode='replace'. File path to edit.

old_string.description: REQUIRED when mode='replace'. Exact text to find and replace. Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include surrounding context lines to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: REQUIRED when mode='replace'. Replacement text. Pass empty string '' to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
REQUIRED when mode='patch'. V4A format patch content. Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts), 'close' (close stdin/send EOF).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
For servers/watchers, do NOT use shell-level background wrappers (nohup/disown/setsid/trailing '&') in foreground mode. Use background=true so Hermes can track lifecycle and output.
After starting a server, verify readiness with a health check or log signal, then run tests in a separate terminal() call. Avoid blind sleep loops.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180, foreground max: 600). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily. Foreground timeout above 600s is rejected; use background=true for longer commands.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified exactly once when the process finishes. **This is the right choice for almost every long-running task** — tests, builds, deployments, multi-item batch jobs, anything that takes over a minute and has a defined end. Use this and keep working on other things; the system notifies you on exit. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with watch_patterns — when both are set, watch_patterns is dropped.

watch_patterns.description: Strings to watch for in background process output. HARD RATE LIMIT: at most 1 notification per 15 seconds per process — matches arriving inside the cooldown are dropped. After 3 consecutive 15-second windows with dropped matches, watch_patterns is automatically disabled for that process and promoted to notify_on_complete behavior (one notification on exit, no more mid-process spam). USE ONLY for truly rare, one-shot mid-process signals on LONG-LIVED processes that will never exit on their own — e.g. ['Application startup complete'] on a server so you know when to hit its endpoint, or ['migration done'] on a daemon. DO NOT use for: (1) end-of-run markers like 'DONE'/'PASS' — use notify_on_complete instead; (2) error patterns like 'ERROR'/'Traceback' in loops or multi-item batch jobs — they fire on every iteration and you'll hit the strike limit fast; (3) anything you'd ever combine with notify_on_complete. When in doubt, choose notify_on_complete. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with notify_on_complete — set one, not both.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits. Auto-runs syntax checks on .py/.json/.yaml/.toml and other linted languages; only NEW errors introduced by this write are surfaced (pre-existing errors are filtered out).

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

{"content": "     1|# google_meet plugin
     2|
     3|Let the hermes agent join a Google Meet call, transcribe it, optionally speak
     4|in it, and do the followup work afterwards.
     5|
     6|## What ships
     7|
     8|| Version | What | Status |
     9||---|---|---|
    10|| v1 | Transcribe-only: Playwright joins Meet, scrapes captions to transcript file | ✓ ships by default |
    11|| v2 | Realtime duplex audio: bot speaks in-call via OpenAI Realtime + BlackHole/PulseAudio null-sink | ✓ opt in with `mode='realtime'` |
    12|| v3 | Remote node host: run the bot on a different machine than the gateway | ✓ opt in with `node='<name>'` |
    13|
    14|## Architecture
    15|
    16|```
    17|┌─ gateway (Linux box, where hermes runs) ────────────────────────────┐
    18|│                                                                      │
    19|│   agent → meet_join(url, mode='realtime', node='my-mac')             │
    20|│         │                                                            │
    21|│         └─ NodeClient ─── ws ────┐                                   │
    22|│                                  │                                   │
    23|└──────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┘
    24|                                   │ wss (token auth)
    25|                                   ▼
    26|┌─ node host (user's Mac, signed-in Chrome lives here) ───────────────┐
    27|│                                                                      │
    28|│   NodeServer (from `hermes meet node run`)                           │
    29|│     │                                                                │
    30|│     ├─ start_bot → process_manager.start() → spawns meet_bot         │
    31|│     │                                                                │
    32|│     └─ meet_bot (Playwright)                                         │
    33|│        ├─ Chromium → meet.google.com                                 │
    34|│        ├─ caption scraper → transcript.txt                           │
    35|│        └─ (realtime mode only) RealtimeSpeaker thread                │
    36|│             ↓                                                        │
    37|│           OpenAI Realtime WS → speaker.pcm                           │
    38|│             ↓                                                        │
    39|│           paplay → null-sink ← Chrome fake mic                       │
    40|│                                                                      │
    41|└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    42|```
    43|
    44|Without v3: the whole right column runs on the gateway machine.
    45|Without v2: the "realtime" path is skipped; transcribe runs alone.
    46|
    47|## Files
    48|
    49|| Path | Purpose |
    50||---|---|
    51|| `plugin.yaml` | manifest |
    52|| `__init__.py` | `register(ctx)` — registers 5 tools + `on_session_end` hook + `hermes meet` CLI |
    53|| `meet_bot.py` | Playwright bot subprocess (standalone, `python -m plugins.google_meet.meet_bot`) |
    54|| `process_manager.py` | local bot lifecycle + `enqueue_say` |
    55|| `tools.py` | agent-facing tools + node-routing helper |
    56|| `cli.py` | `hermes meet setup / auth / join / status / transcript / say / stop / node ...` |
    57|| `audio_bridge.py` | v2: PulseAudio null-sink (Linux) + BlackHole probe (macOS) |
    58|| `realtime/openai_client.py` | v2: `RealtimeSession` + `RealtimeSpeaker` (file-queue → OpenAI Realtime WS → PCM) |
    59|| `node/protocol.py` | v3: message envelope + validation |
    60|| `node/registry.py` | v3: `$HERMES_HOME/workspace/meetings/nodes.json` |
    61|| `node/server.py` | v3: `NodeServer` (runs on host machine) |
    62|| `node/client.py` | v3: `NodeClient` (used by tool handlers + CLI on gateway) |
    63|| `node/cli.py` | v3: `hermes meet node {run,list,approve,remove,status,ping}` |
    64|| `SKILL.md` | agent usage guide |
    65|
    66|## Local quick start
    67|
    68|```bash
    69|hermes plugins enable google_meet
    70|hermes meet install                                      # pip + Chromium
    71|hermes meet setup                                        # preflight
    72|hermes meet auth                                         # optional
    73|hermes meet join https://meet.google.com/abc-defg-hij    # transcribe
    74|```
    75|
    76|## Realtime mode
    77|
    78|Linux (preferred, most automated):
    79|```bash
    80|hermes meet install --realtime                     # installs pulseaudio-utils
    81|echo 'OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...' >> ~/.hermes/.env
    82|hermes meet join https://meet.google.com/abc-defg-hij --mode realtime
    83|# then from the agent or CLI:
    84|hermes meet say "Good morning everyone, I'm the note-taker bot."
    85|```
    86|
    87|macOS:
    88|```bash
    89|hermes meet install --realtime     # runs: brew install blackhole-2ch ffmpeg
    90|# then — manually! — open System Settings → Sound → Input → BlackHole 2ch
    91|echo 'OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...' >> ~/.hermes/.env
    92|hermes meet join https://meet.google.com/abc-defg-hij --mode realtime
    93|```
    94|
    95|On macOS, hermes will **not** switch your system audio input automatically — the
    96|user has to do it. This is deliberate: switching default input on a whim would
    97|be a surprising side effect.
    98|
    99|## Remote node host
   100|
   101|On the node machine (e.g. user's Mac with a signed-in Chrome):
   102|```bash
   103|pip install playwright websockets
   104|python -m playwright install chromium
   105|hermes plugins enable google_meet
   106|hermes meet node run --display-name my-mac --host 0.0.0.0 --port 18789
   107|# prints the bearer token on first run; copy it
   108|```
   109|
   110|On the gateway:
   111|```bash
   112|hermes meet node approve my-mac ws://<mac-ip>:18789 <token>
   113|hermes meet node ping my-mac
   114|# now any meet_* tool call accepts node='my-mac' (or 'auto')
   115|```
   116|
   117|## Safety
   118|
   119|- URL gate: only `https://meet.google.com/abc-defg-hij`, `/new`, `/lookup/<id>`.
   120|- No calendar scanning, no auto-dial, no auto-consent announcement.
   121|- Node server uses bearer-token auth; no key exchange, no TLS termination
   122|  built in — run it on a LAN or behind a reverse proxy you trust.
   123|- One active meeting per (gateway, node) pair. A second `meet_join` leaves the first.
   124|- `meet_say` refuses unless the active meeting was started with `mode='realtime'`.
   125|
   126|## Out of scope
   127|
   128|- **Calendar scanning** — deliberately not implemented. Join URLs must be explicit.
   129|- **Multi-tenant node sharing** — a node serves one gateway at a time.
   130|- **Windows** — audio bridging isn't tested; `register()` no-ops on Windows.
   131|- **System audio input switching on macOS** — user responsibility, not the bot's.

{"content": "     1|---
     2|name: google_meet
     3|description: Join a Google Meet call, transcribe live captions, optionally speak in realtime, and do the followup work afterwards. Use when the user asks the agent to sit in on a meeting, take notes, summarize, respond in-call, or action items from it.
     4|version: 0.2.0
     5|platforms:
     6|  - linux
     7|  - macos
     8|metadata:
     9|  hermes:
    10|    tags: [meetings, google-meet, transcription, realtime-voice]
    11|---
    12|
    13|# google_meet
    14|
    15|## When to use
    16|
    17|The user says any of:
    18|
    19|- "join my Meet at <url>"
    20|- "take notes on this meeting"
    21|- "summarize the meeting and send followups"
    22|- "sit in on my standup"
    23|- "be a bot in this call and speak up when X"
    24|
    25|## Two modes
    26|
    27|| Mode | What the bot does |
    28||---|---|
    29|| `transcribe` (default) | Joins, enables captions, scrapes a transcript. Listen-only. |
    30|| `realtime` | Same as transcribe PLUS speaks into the meeting via OpenAI Realtime. The agent calls `meet_say(text)` and the bot's voice comes out of the call. |
    31|
    32|Pick `realtime` only when the user actually wants the agent to speak. It costs real money (OpenAI Realtime is pay-per-audio-minute) and requires a virtual audio device set up on the machine running the bot.
    33|
    34|## Two locations
    35|
    36|| Location | When |
    37||---|---|
    38|| Local (default) | Gateway machine runs the Playwright bot directly. |
    39|| Remote node (`node="<name>"`) | Bot runs on a different machine that has a signed-in Chrome and (for realtime) a configured audio bridge. Useful when the gateway runs on a headless Linux box but the user's real signed-in Chrome lives on their Mac. |
    40|
    41|## Prerequisites the user must handle once
    42|
    43|Easiest path — run the built-in installer:
    44|
    45|```bash
    46|hermes plugins enable google_meet
    47|hermes meet install                 # pip deps + Chromium (transcribe only)
    48|hermes meet install --realtime      # + pulseaudio-utils / brew blackhole+ffmpeg
    49|hermes meet auth                    # optional; skips guest-lobby wait
    50|hermes meet setup                   # preflight checks
    51|```
    52|
    53|`hermes meet install --realtime` prompts before running `sudo apt-get` (Linux)
    54|or `brew install` (macOS). Pass `--yes` to skip the prompt. It will NOT touch
    55|your macOS default-input setting — you have to select BlackHole 2ch in
    56|System Settings yourself before starting a realtime meeting.
    57|
    58|Or do it manually:
    59|```bash
    60|pip install playwright websockets && python -m playwright install chromium
    61|
    62|# For realtime mode, additionally:
    63|#   Linux:  sudo apt install pulseaudio-utils
    64|#   macOS:  brew install blackhole-2ch ffmpeg
    65|#           → System Settings → Sound → Input → BlackHole 2ch
    66|#   Then set OPENAI_API_KEY or HERMES_MEET_REALTIME_KEY in ~/.hermes/.env
    67|```
    68|
    69|For a remote node:
    70|```bash
    71|# on the user's Mac (where Chrome is signed in):
    72|pip install playwright websockets && python -m playwright install chromium
    73|hermes plugins enable google_meet
    74|hermes meet node run --display-name my-mac    # persistent server
    75|# copy the printed token
    76|
    77|# on the gateway:
    78|hermes meet node approve my-mac ws://<mac-ip>:18789 <token>
    79|hermes meet node ping my-mac                   # confirm reachable
    80|```
    81|
    82|Run `hermes meet setup` to preflight local prereqs.
    83|
    84|## Flow
    85|
    86|1. **Join** — call `meet_join(url=..., mode=..., node=...)`. Returns immediately.
    87|2. **Announce yourself** — no auto-consent. Say (in whatever channel the user is watching): "A Hermes agent bot is in this call taking notes."
    88|3. **Poll** — `meet_status()` for liveness, `meet_transcript(last=20)` for recent captions. Don't re-read the whole transcript every turn.
    89|4. **Speak (realtime only)** — `meet_say(text="...")` queues text for TTS. The speech lags by ~2s. Don't spam it.
    90|5. **Leave** — `meet_leave()` when done, or set `duration="30m"` on `meet_join` for auto-leave.
    91|6. **Follow up** — read `meet_transcript()` in full, summarize, and use regular tools to send the recap, file issues, schedule followups.
    92|
    93|## Tool reference
    94|
    95|| Tool | Parameters | Use |
    96||---|---|---|
    97|| `meet_join` | `url`, `mode?`, `guest_name?`, `duration?`, `headed?`, `node?` | Start bot |
    98|| `meet_status` | `node?` | Liveness + progress |
    99|| `meet_transcript` | `last?`, `node?` | Read captions |
   100|| `meet_leave` | `node?` | Close bot |
   101|| `meet_say` | `text`, `node?` | Speak in realtime meeting |
   102|
   103|`node?` on all tools: pass a registered node name (or `"auto"` for the sole node) to operate a remote bot instead of a local one. Omit for local.
   104|
   105|## Important limits
   106|
   107|- Captions are only as good as Google Meet's live captions. English-biased, lossy on overlapping speakers.
   108|- Guest mode sits in the lobby until a host admits. Warn the user; `hermes meet auth` avoids this.
   109|- **Lobby timeout**: if the host doesn't admit the bot within 5 minutes (configurable via `HERMES_MEET_LOBBY_TIMEOUT` env), the bot leaves and `meet_status` reports `leaveReason: "lobby_timeout"`.
   110|- **One active meeting per install per location.** A second `meet_join` leaves the first.
   111|- **Windows not supported.**
   112|- Realtime mode needs a virtual audio device. If the audio bridge setup fails, the bot falls back to transcribe mode and flags it in `meet_status().error`.
   113|- `meet_say` requires `mode='realtime'` on the originating `meet_join`. Calling it against a transcribe-mode meeting returns a clear error.
   114|- **Barge-in is best-effort.** When a caption arrives attributed to a real participant while the bot is generating audio, the bot sends `response.cancel` to OpenAI Realtime. Captions take ~500ms to show up, so the bot will talk over the first second or so of a human interruption.
   115|
   116|## Status dict reference
   117|
   118|`meet_status()` returns (subset shown, there are more):
   119|
   120|| Key | Meaning |
   121||---|---|
   122|| `inCall` | Past the lobby. False while waiting for admission. |
   123|| `lobbyWaiting` | Clicked "Ask to join", waiting on host. |
   124|| `joinAttemptedAt` / `joinedAt` | Timestamps for lobby-click and actual admission. |
   125|| `captioning` | Caption observer is installed. |
   126|| `transcriptLines` / `lastCaptionAt` | Transcript progress. |
   127|| `realtime` / `realtimeReady` | Realtime mode provisioned / WS connected. |
   128|| `realtimeDevice` | Audio device name the bot is feeding (e.g. `hermes_meet_src`). |
   129|| `audioBytesOut` / `lastAudioOutAt` | How much PCM the OpenAI session has produced. |
   130|| `lastBargeInAt` | Timestamp of the most recent `response.cancel` sent. |
   131|| `leaveReason` | `duration_expired`, `lobby_timeout`, `denied`, `page_closed`, or null. |
   132|| `error` | Last error (soft — bot may still be running). |
   133|
   134|## Transcript location
   135|
   136|Local:
   137|```
   138|$HERMES_HOME/workspace/meetings/<meeting-id>/transcript.txt
   139|```
   140|
   141|Remote node: transcript lives on the node host's disk. Use `meet_transcript(node=...)` to read it over RPC.
   142|
   143|## Safety
   144|
   145|- URL regex: only `https://meet.google.com/...` URLs pass.
   146|- No calendar scanning. No auto-dial.
   147|- Remote nodes use bearer-token auth; tokens are generated on the node (32 hex chars, persisted in `$HERMES_HOME/workspace/meetings/node_token.json`) and must be copied to the gateway via `hermes meet node approve`.
   148|- `meet_say` text is rate-limited by the OpenAI Realtime session; spam-protection is the bot's problem, not yours, but still — don't queue hundreds of lines.
   149|", "total_lines": 148, "file_size": 7069, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|"""google_meet plugin — let the agent join a Meet call, transcribe it, follow up.
     2|
     3|v1: transcribe-only. Spawns a headless Chromium via Playwright, joins the Meet
     4|URL, enables live captions, scrapes them into a transcript file. The agent then
     5|has the transcript in its workspace and can do whatever followup work it needs
     6|using its regular tools.
     7|
     8|v2 (not in this PR): realtime duplex audio so the agent can speak in the
     9|meeting, via OpenAI Realtime / Gemini Live + BlackHole / PulseAudio null-sink.
    10|``meet_say`` exists as a stub today so the tool surface is stable.
    11|
    12|Explicit-by-design: only joins ``https://meet.google.com/`` URLs explicitly
    13|passed in. No calendar scanning, no auto-dial, no consent announcement.
    14|"""
    15|
    16|from __future__ import annotations
    17|
    18|import logging
    19|import platform
    20|
    21|from plugins.google_meet import process_manager as pm
    22|from plugins.google_meet.cli import register_cli as _register_meet_cli
    23|from plugins.google_meet.cli import meet_command as _meet_command
    24|from plugins.google_meet.tools import (
    25|    MEET_JOIN_SCHEMA,
    26|    MEET_LEAVE_SCHEMA,
    27|    MEET_SAY_SCHEMA,
    28|    MEET_STATUS_SCHEMA,
    29|    MEET_TRANSCRIPT_SCHEMA,
    30|    check_meet_requirements,
    31|    handle_meet_join,
    32|    handle_meet_leave,
    33|    handle_meet_say,
    34|    handle_meet_status,
    35|    handle_meet_transcript,
    36|)
    37|
    38|logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    39|
    40|
    41|_TOOLS = (
    42|    ("meet_join",       MEET_JOIN_SCHEMA,       handle_meet_join,       "📞"),
    43|    ("meet_status",     MEET_STATUS_SCHEMA,     handle_meet_status,     "🟢"),
    44|    ("meet_transcript", MEET_TRANSCRIPT_SCHEMA, handle_meet_transcript, "📝"),
    45|    ("meet_leave",      MEET_LEAVE_SCHEMA,      handle_meet_leave,      "👋"),
    46|    ("meet_say",        MEET_SAY_SCHEMA,        handle_meet_say,        "🗣️"),
    47|)
    48|
    49|
    50|def _on_session_end(**kwargs) -> None:
    51|    """Best-effort cleanup — if a meet bot is still running when the session
    52|    ends, leave the call so we don't orphan a headless Chromium.
    53|
    54|    No-ops when nothing is active. Swallows all exceptions — session end must
    55|    not fail because the bot cleanup hit an edge case.
    56|    """
    57|    try:
    58|        status = pm.status()
    59|        if status.get("ok") and status.get("alive"):
    60|            pm.stop(reason="session ended")
    61|    except Exception as e:  # pragma: no cover — defensive
    62|        logger.debug("google_meet on_session_end cleanup failed: %s", e)
    63|
    64|
    65|def register(ctx) -> None:
    66|    """Register tools, CLI, and lifecycle hooks.
    67|
    68|    Called once by the plugin loader when the plugin is enabled via
    69|    ``plugins.enabled`` in config.yaml.
    70|    """
    71|    # Windows is not supported in v1 — audio routing for v2 doesn't have a
    72|    # tested path there and guest-join Chromium is flakier. Refuse to register
    73|    # rather than half-working.
    74|    system = platform.system().lower()
    75|    if system not in ("linux", "darwin"):
    76|        logger.info(
    77|            "google_meet plugin: platform=%s not supported (linux/macos only)",
    78|            system,
    79|        )
    80|        return
    81|
    82|    for name, schema, handler, emoji in _TOOLS:
    83|        ctx.register_tool(
    84|            name=name,
    85|            toolset="google_meet",
    86|            schema=schema,
    87|            handler=handler,
    88|            check_fn=check_meet_requirements,
    89|            emoji=emoji,
    90|        )
    91|
    92|    ctx.register_cli_command(
    93|        name="meet",
    94|        help="Google Meet bot (join, transcribe, follow up)",
    95|        setup_fn=_register_meet_cli,
    96|        handler_fn=_meet_command,
    97|        description=(
    98|            "Let the hermes agent join a Google Meet call and scrape live "
    99|            "captions into a transcript. See: hermes meet setup"
   100|        ),
   101|    )
   102|
   103|    ctx.register_hook("on_session_end", _on_session_end)

   215|def _cmd_install(*, realtime: bool, assume_yes: bool) -> int:
   216|    """Install the plugin's prerequisites.
   217|
   218|    Always: pip install playwright + websockets, then
   219|    ``python -m playwright install chromium``.
   220|
   221|    With ``--realtime``: also install the platform audio bridge deps.
   222|      Linux : ``sudo apt-get install -y pulseaudio-utils``

   232|

   234|    if system not in ("Linux", "Darwin"):
   235|        print(f"google_meet install: {system} is not supported (linux/macos only)")
   236|        return 1

   238|    def _confirm(prompt: str) -> bool:

   247|    print("google_meet install")
   248|    print("-------------------")
   249|
   250|    # 1) pip deps — always safe, venv-scoped.
   251|    pip_pkgs = ["playwright", "websockets"]
   252|    print(f"\n[1/3] pip install: {' '.join(pip_pkgs)}")
   253|    try:
   254|        res = _sp.run(

   257|        )
   258|        if res.returncode != 0:
   259|            print("  pip install failed")

   262|        print(f"  pip install failed: {e}")
   263|        return 1
   264|
   265|    # 2) Playwright browsers — pulls chromium (~300MB first run).
   266|    print("\n[2/3] python -m playwright install chromium")
   267|    try:
   268|        res = _sp.run(

   270|            check=False,
   271|        )
   272|        if res.returncode != 0:

   276|        return 1

   289|                else:

   291|                    print(f"  $ {' '.join(cmd)}")
   292|                    res = _sp.run(cmd, check=False)

{"content": "     1|"""Headless Google Meet bot — Playwright + live-caption scraping.
     2|
     3|Runs as a standalone subprocess spawned by ``process_manager.py``. Reads config
     4|from env vars, writes status + transcript to files under
     5|``$HERMES_HOME/workspace/meetings/<meeting-id>/``. The main hermes process
     6|reads those files via the ``meet_*`` tools — no IPC beyond filesystem.
     7|
     8|The scraping strategy mirrors OpenUtter (sumansid/openutter): we don't parse
     9|WebRTC audio, we enable Google Meet's built-in live captions and observe the
    10|captions container in the DOM via a MutationObserver. This is lossy and
    11|English-biased but it is:
    12|
    13|* deterministic (no API keys, no STT billing),
    14|* works behind Meet's normal login / admission,
    15|* survives Meet UI rewrites fairly well because the caption container has a
    16|  stable ARIA role.
    17|
    18|Run standalone for debugging::
    19|
    20|    HERMES_MEET_URL=https://meet.google.com/abc-defg-hij \\
    21|    HERMES_MEET_OUT_DIR=/tmp/meet-debug \\
    22|    HERMES_MEET_HEADED=1 \\
    23|    python -m plugins.google_meet.meet_bot

    26|``https://meet.google.com/`` is rejected (explicit-by-design).

    41|# Match ``https://meet.google.com/abc-defg-hij`` or ``.../lookup/...`` — the
    42|# short three-segment code or a lookup URL. Anything else is rejected.

    44|    r"^https://meet\.google\.com/("
    45|    r"[a-z0-9]{3,}-[a-z0-9]{3,}-[a-z0-9]{3,}"

    64|def _meeting_id_from_url(url: str) -> str:

    69|    bot won't know the real code until after redirect, and callers pass this
    70|    through to filename anyway.

   143|            "meetingId": self.meeting_id,
   144|            "url": self.url,

   146|            "captioning": self.captioning,

   161|            "audioBytesOut": self.audio_bytes_out,
   162|            "lastAudioOutAt": self.last_audio_out_at,
   163|            "lastBargeInAt": self.last_barge_in_at,
   164|            "leaveReason": self.leave_reason,

   166|        tmp = self.status_path.with_suffix(".json.tmp")
   167|        tmp.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
   168|        tmp.replace(self.status_path)

   179|
   180|# JavaScript injected into the Meet tab to observe captions. Captures

   183|# mirrors the OpenUtter caption scraping approach.
   184|_CAPTION_OBSERVER_JS = r"""
   185|(() => {
   186|  if (window.__hermesMeetInstalled) return;
   187|  window.__hermesMeetInstalled = true;
   188|  window.__hermesMeetQueue = [];

   198|      speaker: (speaker || '').trim(),
   199|      text: text.trim(),
   200|    });
   201|  }
   202|
   203|  function scan(root) {
   204|    // Meet captions render as a list of rows; each row contains a speaker
   205|    // label and a text block. Selectors vary across Meet rewrites; we try
   206|    // a few shapes and fall back to raw text.
   207|    const rows = root.querySelectorAll('div[jsname="dsyhDe"], div.CNusmb, div.TBMuR');

   209|      rows.forEach((row) => {
   210|        const spkEl = row.querySelector('div.KcIKyf, div.zs7s8d, span[jsname="YSxPC"]');
   211|        const txtEl = row.querySelector('div.bh44bd, span[jsname="tgaKEf"], div.iTTPOb');

   213|        const text = txtEl ? txtEl.innerText : row.innerText;
   214|        pushEntry(speaker, text);
   215|      });
   216|      return;
   217|    }
   218|    // Fallback: treat the whole region's innerText as one anonymous line.
   219|    const text = (root.innerText || '').split('\n').filter(Boolean).pop();
   220|    pushEntry('', text);
   221|  }
   222|
   223|  function attach() {
   224|    const el = document.querySelector(captionSelector);
   225|    if (!el) return false;
   226|    const obs = new MutationObserver(() => scan(el));
   227|    obs.observe(el, { childList: true, subtree: true, characterData: true });
   228|    scan(el);
   229|    return true;
   230|  }
   231|
   232|  // Try now and retry on interval — the caption region only appears after
   233|  // captions are enabled and someone speaks.
   234|  if (!attach()) {
   235|    const iv = setInterval(() => { if (attach()) clearInterval(iv); }, 1500);
   236|  }
   237|
   238|  window.__hermesMeetDrain = () => {
   239|    const out = window.__hermesMeetQueue.slice();
   240|    window.__hermesMeetQueue = [];
   241|    return out;
   242|  };
   243|})();

   265|def _start_realtime_speaker(

   341|    # PCM pump: feeds speaker.pcm (24kHz s16le mono) into the OS audio

   356|                    "--format=s16le",
   357|                    "--channels=1",
   358|                    f"--device={sink}",

   361|                stdin=_sp.DEVNULL,
   362|                stdout=_sp.DEVNULL,
   363|                stderr=_sp.DEVNULL,
   364|            )

   368|    elif platform_tag == "darwin":

   374|        # the file in a tight loop if ffmpeg is absent.
   375|        import shutil as _shutil
   376|        import subprocess as _sp
   377|
   378|        device_name = (bridge_info or {}).get("write_target") or "BlackHole 2ch"

   382|                # -f avfoundation -i: speaker path as raw PCM.
   383|                # -f s16le -ar 24000 -ac 1 -i <pcm>: interpret the file.

   386|                # ffmpeg's audiotoolbox output picks the current default
   387|                # output device, which isn't what we want. Instead we use
   388|                # -f avfoundation with the named device as OUTPUT via
   389|                # -vn and the device name.
   390|                proc = _sp.Popen(
   391|                    [
   392|                        "ffmpeg",
   393|                        "-nostdin", "-hide_banner", "-loglevel", "error",
   394|                        "-re",
   395|                        "-f", "s16le", "-ar", "24000", "-ac", "1",
   396|                        "-i", str(pcm_path),
   397|                        "-f", "audiotoolbox",
   398|                        "-audio_device_index", _mac_audio_device_index(device_name),
   399|                        "-",
   400|                    ],
   401|                    stdin=_sp.DEVNULL,
   402|                    stdout=_sp.DEVNULL,
   403|                    stderr=_sp.DEVNULL,
   404|                )
   405|                rt["pcm_pump"] = proc
   406|            except FileNotFoundError:

   408|            except Exception as e:
   409|                state.set(error=f"macOS pcm pump failed to start: {e}")
   410|        else:

   414|def _mac_audio_device_index(device_name: str) -> str:
   415|    """Return the ffmpeg ``-audio_device_index`` for *device_name*, as a string.
   416|
   417|    Probes ``ffmpeg -f avfoundation -list_devices true -i ''`` (which prints
   418|    the device table on stderr) and matches *device_name* case-insensitively.
   419|    Defaults to ``"0"`` if the device can't be found — caller will get a
   420|    misrouted stream but not a crash, and the error will be obvious.
   421|    """
   422|    import subprocess as _sp
   423|

   425|        out = _sp.run(
   426|            ["ffmpeg", "-f", "avfoundation", "-list_devices", "true", "-i", ""],
   427|            capture_output=True,
   428|            text=True,
   429|            timeout=10,
   430|        )
   431|    except Exception:

   433|    # ffmpeg prints the table on stderr. Lines look like:
   434|    #   [AVFoundation indev @ 0x...] [0] BlackHole 2ch

{"content": "     1|"""Subprocess lifecycle manager for the google_meet bot.
     2|
     3|Single active meeting at a time. Stores the running pid + out_dir in a
     4|session-scoped state file under ``$HERMES_HOME/workspace/meetings/.active.json``
     5|so tool calls across turns can find the bot, and ``on_session_end`` can clean
     6|it up.
     7|
     8|The bot runs as a detached subprocess — we don't hold file descriptors open,
     9|so the parent agent loop can't block on it. We communicate via files only.
    10|"""
    11|
    12|from __future__ import annotations
    13|
    14|import json
    15|import os
    16|import signal
    17|import subprocess
    18|import sys
    19|import time
    20|from pathlib import Path
    21|from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
    22|
    23|from hermes_constants import get_hermes_home
    24|
    25|# File + directory layout (under $HERMES_HOME):
    26|#
    27|#   workspace/meetings/
    28|#       .active.json                # pointer to current session's bot
    29|#       <meeting-id>/
    30|#           status.json             # live bot state (written by bot each tick)
    31|#           transcript.txt          # scraped captions
    32|#
    33|# .active.json holds:
    34|#   {"pid": 12345, "meeting_id": "abc-defg-hij", "out_dir": "...",
    35|#    "url": "https://meet.google.com/...", "started_at": [REDACTED_PHONE].0,
    36|#    "session_id": "optional"}
    37|
    38|
    39|def _root() -> Path:
    40|    return Path(get_hermes_home()) / "workspace" / "meetings"
    41|
    42|
    43|def _active_file() -> Path:
    44|    return _root() / ".active.json"
    45|
    46|
    47|def _read_active() -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
    48|    p = _active_file()
    49|    if not p.is_file():
    50|        return None
    51|    try:
    52|        return json.loads(p.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    53|    except Exception:
    54|        return None
    55|
    56|
    57|def _write_active(data: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    58|    p = _active_file()
    59|    p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    60|    tmp = p.with_suffix(".json.tmp")
    61|    tmp.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
    62|    tmp.replace(p)
    63|
    64|
    65|def _clear_active() -> None:
    66|    try:
    67|        _active_file().unlink()
    68|    except FileNotFoundError:
    69|        pass
    70|
    71|
    72|def _pid_alive(pid: int) -> bool:
    73|    # ``os.kill(pid, 0)`` is NOT a no-op on Windows (bpo-14484) — it
    74|    # routes through GenerateConsoleCtrlEvent and can kill the target.
    75|    # Use the cross-platform existence check.
    76|    from gateway.status import _pid_exists
    77|    return _pid_exists(pid)
    78|
    79|
    80|# [REDACTED_TOKEN]------------
    81|# Public API — used by tool handlers + CLI
    82|# [REDACTED_TOKEN]------------
    83|
    84|def start(
    85|    url: str,
    86|    *,
    87|    out_dir: Optional[Path] = None,
    88|    headed: bool = False,
    89|    auth_state: Optional[str] = None,
    90|    guest_name: str = "Hermes Agent",
    91|    duration: Optional[str] = None,
    92|    session_id: Optional[str] = None,
    93|    mode: str = "transcribe",
    94|    realtime_model: Optional[str] = None,
    95|    realtime_voice: Optional[str] = None,
    96|    realtime_instructions: Optional[str] = None,
    97|    realtime_api_key: Optional[str] = None,
    98|) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    99|    """Spawn the meet_bot subprocess for *url*.
   100|
   101|    If a bot is already running for this hermes install, leave it first —
   102|    we enforce single-active-meeting semantics.
   103|
   104|    Returns a dict summarizing the started bot.
   105|    """
   106|    from plugins.google_meet.meet_bot import _is_safe_meet_url, _meeting_id_from_url
   107|
   108|    if not _is_safe_meet_url(url):
   109|        return {
   110|            "ok": False,
   111|            "error": (
   112|                "refusing: only https://meet.google.com/ URLs are allowed. "
   113|                "got: " + repr(url)
   114|            ),
   115|        }
   116|
   117|    existing = _read_active()
   118|    if existing and _pid_alive(int(existing.get("pid", 0))):
   119|        stop(reason="replaced by new meet_join")
   120|
   121|    meeting_id = _meeting_id_from_url(url)
   122|    out = out_dir or (_root() / meeting_id)
   123|    out.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   124|
   125|    # Wipe any stale transcript/status files from a previous run of this
   126|    # meeting id so polling isn't confused.
   127|    for name in ("transcript.txt", "status.json"):
   128|        f = out / name
   129|        if f.exists():
   130|            try:
   131|                f.unlink()
   132|            except OSError:
   133|                pass
   134|
   135|    env = os.environ.copy()
   136|    env["HERMES_MEET_URL"] = url
   137|    env["HERMES_MEET_OUT_DIR"] = str(out)
   138|    env["HERMES_MEET_GUEST_NAME"] = guest_name
   139|    if headed:
   140|        env["HERMES_MEET_HEADED"] = "1"
   141|    if auth_state:
   142|        env["HERMES_MEET_AUTH_STATE"] = auth_state
   143|    if duration:
   144|        env["HERMES_MEET_DURATION"] = duration
   145|    # v2: realtime mode + passthroughs. The bot defaults to transcribe
   146|    # mode if HERMES_MEET_MODE isn't set, matching v1 behavior.
   147|    if mode:
   148|        env["HERMES_MEET_MODE"] = mode
   149|    if realtime_model:
   150|        env["HERMES_MEET_REALTIME_MODEL"] = realtime_model
   151|    if realtime_voice:
   152|        env["HERMES_MEET_REALTIME_VOICE"] = realtime_voice
   153|    if realtime_instructions:
   154|        env["HERMES_MEET_REALTIME_INSTRUCTIONS"] = realtime_instructions
   155|    if realtime_api_key:
   156|        env["HERMES_MEET_REALTIME_KEY"] = realtime_api_key
   157|
   158|    log_path = out / "bot.log"
   159|    # Detach: stdin=devnull, stdout/stderr → log file, new session so parent
   160|    # signals don't propagate.
   161|    log_fh = open(log_path, "ab", buffering=0)
   162|    try:
   163|        proc = subprocess.Popen(
   164|            [sys.executable, "-m", "plugins.google_meet.meet_bot"],
   165|            stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   166|            stdout=log_fh,
   167|            stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
   168|            env=env,
   169|            start_new_session=True,
   170|            close_fds=True,
   171|        )
   172|    finally:
   173|        # The subprocess now owns the log fd; we can close ours.
   174|        log_fh.close()
   175|

{"content": "     1|"""Virtual audio bridge for feeding generated speech into Chrome's mic.
     2|
     3|v2 module. Provisions a platform-specific virtual audio device so the
     4|Meet bot's Chromium instance can be pointed at an input source we
     5|control. The OpenAI Realtime client writes PCM bytes into this device;
     6|Chrome reads them as if they were coming from a microphone.
     7|
     8|Linux (primary): uses pactl (PulseAudio) to create a null-sink plus a
     9|virtual source whose master is the null-sink's monitor. Callers set
    10|PULSE_SOURCE=<source_name> in Chrome's env and pass the fake-mic flag.
    11|
    12|macOS: requires BlackHole 2ch to be installed. This module only
    13|verifies its presence and returns the device name; routing OS default
    14|input is left to the user (or a future switchaudio-osx integration) to
    15|avoid surprising the user's system audio state.
    16|
    17|Windows: not supported in v2.
    18|"""
    19|
    20|from __future__ import annotations
    21|
    22|import platform
    23|import subprocess
    24|from typing import Optional
    25|
    26|
    27|_BLACKHOLE_DEVICE = "BlackHole 2ch"
    28|
    29|
    30|class AudioBridge:
    31|    """Manages a virtual audio device for Chrome fake-mic input.
    32|
    33|    Call ``setup()`` once before launching the Meet bot and
    34|    ``teardown()`` when the session ends. ``teardown()`` is idempotent.
    35|    """
    36|
    37|    def __init__(self, name_prefix: str = "hermes_meet") -> None:
    38|        self._name_prefix = name_prefix
    39|        self._platform: Optional[str] = None
    40|        self._device_name: Optional[str] = None
    41|        self._write_target: Optional[str] = None
    42|        self._module_ids: list[int] = []
    43|        self._torn_down = False
    44|
    45|    # ── public properties ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
    46|
    47|    @property
    48|    def device_name(self) -> str:
    49|        if not self._device_name:
    50|            raise RuntimeError("AudioBridge not set up yet")
    51|        return self._device_name
    52|
    53|    @property
    54|    def write_target(self) -> str:
    55|        if not self._write_target:
    56|            raise RuntimeError("AudioBridge not set up yet")
    57|        return self._write_target
    58|
    59|    # ── lifecycle ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    60|
    61|    def setup(self) -> dict:
    62|        """Provision the virtual audio device.
    63|
    64|        Returns a dict describing the device. Raises RuntimeError on
    65|        unsupported platforms or when required system tools are missing.
    66|        """
    67|        system = platform.system()
    68|        if system == "Linux":
    69|            return self._setup_linux()
    70|        if system == "Darwin":
    71|            return self._setup_darwin()
    72|        if system == "Windows":
    73|            raise RuntimeError("windows not supported in v2")
    74|        raise RuntimeError(f"unsupported platform: {system}")
    75|
    76|    def teardown(self) -> None:
    77|        """Release the virtual audio device. Idempotent."""
    78|        if self._torn_down:
    79|            return
    80|        # Only Linux needs explicit unloading.
    81|        if self._platform == "linux" and self._module_ids:
    82|            # Unload in reverse order (virtual-source before null-sink).
    83|            for mod_id in reversed(self._module_ids):
    84|                try:
    85|                    subprocess.run(
    86|                        ["pactl", "unload-module", str(mod_id)],
    87|                        check=False,
    88|                        capture_output=True,
    89|                    )
    90|                except Exception:
    91|                    # Best-effort teardown — never raise from here.
    92|                    pass
    93|            self._module_ids = []
    94|        self._torn_down = True
    95|
    96|    # ── platform impls ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    97|
    98|    def _setup_linux(self) -> dict:
    99|        sink_name = f"{self._name_prefix}_sink"
   100|        src_name = f"{self._name_prefix}_src"
   101|
   102|        try:
   103|            sink_out = subprocess.run(
   104|                [
   105|                    "pactl",
   106|                    "load-module",
   107|                    "module-null-sink",
   108|                    f"sink_name={sink_name}",
   109|                    f"sink_properties=device.description=HermesMeetSink",
   110|                ],
   111|                check=True,
   112|                capture_output=True,
   113|                text=True,
   114|            )
   115|        except FileNotFoundError as exc:
   116|            raise RuntimeError(
   117|                "pactl not found — install PulseAudio/pipewire-pulse"
   118|            ) from exc
   119|        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as exc:
   120|            raise RuntimeError(
   121|                f"pactl load-module null-sink failed: {exc.stderr or exc}"
   122|            ) from exc
   123|
   124|        sink_mod_id = self._parse_module_id(sink_out.stdout)
   125|
   126|        try:
   127|            src_out = subprocess.run(
   128|                [
   129|                    "pactl",
   130|                    "load-module",
   131|                    "module-virtual-source",
   132|                    f"source_name={src_name}",
   133|                    f"master={sink_name}.monitor",
   134|                ],
   135|                check=True,
   136|                capture_output=True,
   137|                text=True,
   138|            )
   139|        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as exc:
   140|            # Roll back the null-sink we just created so we don't leak it.
   141|            subprocess.run(
   142|                ["pactl", "unload-module", str(sink_mod_id)],
   143|                check=False,
   144|                capture_output=True,
   145|            )
   146|            raise RuntimeError(
   147|                f"pactl load-module virtual-source failed: {exc.stderr or exc}"
   148|            ) from exc
   149|
   150|        src_mod_id = self._parse_module_id(src_out.stdout)
   151|
   152|        self._platform = "linux"
   153|        self._device_name = src_name
   154|        self._write_target = sink_name
   155|        self._module_ids = [sink_mod_id, src_mod_id]
   156|        self._torn_down = False
   157|
   158|        return {
   159|            "platform": "linux",
   160|            "device_name": src_name,
   161|            "sample_rate": 48000,
   162|            "channels": 2,
   163|            "module_ids": list(self._module_ids),
   164|            "write_target": sink_name,
   165|        }
   166|
   167|    def _setup_darwin(self) -> dict:
   168|        try:
   169|            out = subprocess.check_output(
   170|                ["system_profiler", "SPAudioDataType"],
   171|                text=True,
   172|                stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
   173|            )
   174|        except FileNotFoundError as exc:
   175|            raise RuntimeError(
   176|                "system_profiler not found (macOS-only command)"
   177|            ) from exc
   178|        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as exc:
   179|            raise RuntimeError(
   180|                f"system_profiler failed: {exc.output}"
   181|            ) from exc
   182|
   183|        if "BlackHole" not in out:
   184|            raise RuntimeError(
   185|                "BlackHole virtual audio device not installed. "
   186|                "Install via: brew install blackhole-2ch"
   187|            )
   188|
   189|        self._platform = "darwin"
   190|        self._device_name = _BLACKHOLE_DEVICE
   191|        self._write_target = _BLACKHOLE_DEVICE
   192|        self._module_ids = []
   193|        self._torn_down = False
   194|
   195|        return {
   196|            "platform": "darwin",
   197|            "device_name": _BLACKHOLE_DEVICE,
   198|            "sample_rate": 48000,
   199|            "channels": 2,
   200|            "module_ids": [],
   201|            "write_target": _BLACKHOLE_DEVICE,
   202|        }
   203|
   204|    # ── helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   205|
   206|    @staticmethod
   207|    def _parse_module_id(stdout: str) -> int:
   208|        """pactl load-module prints the new module ID to stdout."""
   209|        text = (stdout or "").strip()
   210|        if not text:
   211|            raise RuntimeError("pactl load-module returned empty stdout")
   212|        # Take the last whitespace-separated token on the first non-empty line.
   213|        first = text.splitlines()[0].strip()
   214|        token = first.split()[-1]
   215|        try:
   216|            return int(token)
   217|        except ValueError as exc:
   218|            raise RuntimeError(
   219|                f"could not parse pactl module id from: {stdout!r}"
   220|            ) from exc
   221|
   222|
   223|def chrome_fake_audio_flags(bridge_info: dict) -> list[str]:
   224|    """Return Chrome flags for using the fake audio input.
   225|
   226|    The PulseAudio source is selected via the ``PULSE_SOURCE`` env var,
   227|    which callers must set in Chrome's environment before launch:
   228|
   229|        env["PULSE_SOURCE"] = bridge_info["device_name"]
   230|
   231|    On macOS the caller must ensure the system default audio input is
   232|    set to the returned BlackHole device (we do not flip that switch).
   233|    """
   234|    system = platform.system()
   235|    if system == "Linux":
   236|        # Chromium on Linux picks up the PulseAudio source selected via
   237|        # PULSE_SOURCE env var; the fake-ui flag skips the permission
   238|        # prompt so the bot can pick "use my mic" without user input.
   239|        return ["--use-fake-ui-for-media-stream"]
   240|    if system == "Darwin":
   241|        return ["--use-fake-ui-for-media-stream"]
   242|    if system == "Windows":
   243|        raise RuntimeError("windows not supported in v2")
   244|    raise RuntimeError(f"unsupported platform: {system}")

### Configuration Requirements Per Feature

#### Mode 1: Transcribe-only (v1 — basic, works NOW)
**What it does:** Joins meeting, scrapes live captions, writes transcript.
**Already works:** Yes, no additional config needed.
```
hermes meet join https://meet.google.com/abc-defg-hij
```
- Joins as guest → sits in lobby until host admits
- `hermes meet auth` (optional) avoids guest lobby by saving Google sign-in state

#### Mode 2: Realtime Duplex Audio (v2 — bot can speak)
**What it does:** Same as transcribe PLUS bot can talk into the meeting.
**Additional requirements:**
1. **Install audio bridge:** `hermes meet install --realtime` (runs `sudo apt-get install -y pulseaudio-utils`)
2. **API key already set** — `OPENAI_API_KEY` is in `~/.hermes/.env` (also checks `HERMES_MEET_REALTIME_KEY`)
3. On Linux, PulseAudio null-sink is auto-provisioned by the plugin
```
hermes meet join https://meet.google.com/abc-defg-hij --mode realtime
hermes meet say "Good morning everyone"
```

#### Mode 3: Remote Node Host (v3 — bot on a different machine)
**What it does:** Run the Playwright bot on a user's Mac (with signed-in Chrome) while the gateway is elsewhere.
**Setup:**
- On node machine: `pip install playwright websockets && python -m playwright install chromium`
- On node machine: `hermes meet node run --display-name my-mac`
- On gateway: `hermes meet node approve my-mac ws://<mac-ip>:18789 <token>`

### Available CLI Commands
```
hermes meet setup          # Preflight check
hermes meet install        # Install deps (--realtime for audio)
hermes meet auth           # Save Google sign-in (avoids guest lobby)
hermes meet join <url>     # Join a meeting (--mode realtime, --duration 30m, --headed, --node)
hermes meet status         # Bot state + transcript progress
hermes meet transcript     # Read transcript (--last N)
hermes meet say <text>     # Speak in-meeting (realtime mode only)
hermes meet stop           # Leave meeting
hermes meet node run/approve/list/remove/status/ping  # Remote node management
```

### How to Join a Meeting from Chat (Agent Tools)
The plugin provides 5 tools the agent can call:
- `meet_join(url, mode?, guest_name?, duration?, headed?, node?)` — Start bot
- `meet_status(node?)` — Check liveness + progress
- `meet_transcript(last?, node?)` — Read captions
- `meet_leave(node?)` — Close bot
- `meet_say(text, node?)` — Speak (only in realtime mode)
